pubmed_links: [Zufferey et al. (2012) A 600 kb deletion syndrome at 16p11.2 leads to energy imbalance and neuropsychiatric disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22614287/); [D'Angelo et al. (2016) Defining the effect of the 16p11.2 duplication on cognition, behavior, and medical comorbidities](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27410714/)
summary: PPP4C maps within the 16p11.2 chromosomal region, deletions of which are among the most frequent copy-number variant causes of autism spectrum disorder and intellectual disability; PPP4C encodes a serine/threonine phosphatase that regulates microtubule organization. (emerging evidence)
